channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: South Korean investigators seek to question reluctant president over martial law
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — South Korean investigators on Friday again sought to question impeached President Yoon Suk Yeol over his Dec. 3 declaration of martial law, summoning him for questioning on Christmas Day despite his repeated refusal to cooperate.
item.dc:creator: KIM TONG-HYUNG, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: For some, the pope's big Holy Year only aggravates housing crisis as overtourism pushes out Romans
item.description: When Pope Francis formally opens the Holy Year next week, he will launch a dizzying 12-month calendar of events that include special Jubilee Masses for the faithful from all walks of life.
item.dc:creator: Nicole Winfield And Isaia Montelione, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: EU leaders insist no decisions can be taken about Ukraine without Ukraine, or behind their backs
item.description: BRUSSELS (AP) — European Union leaders insisted on Thursday that no decisions can be taken about the future of war-ravaged Ukraine without its consent — or behind the backs of its partners in Europe, barely a month before President-elect Donald Trump takes office.
item.dc:creator: LORNE COOK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Biden to travel to Italy for meetings with pope, Meloni in January
item.description: President Biden will travel to Italy in January for meetings with Pope Francis, Italian Prime Minister Giorgia Meloni and Italian President Sergio Mattarella. The trip, from Jan. 9 to 12, will likely be his last foreign trip as president before President-elect Trump is sworn in on Jan. 20.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, The Hill

item.title: Israel and Hamas appear close to a ceasefire deal. These are the sticking points
item.description: CAIRO (AP) — Israel and Hamas appear closer than they have been in months to agreeing to a ceasefire that could wind down the 14-month war in Gaza and bring home dozens of people held hostage there. But the sides have come close before, only to have talks collapse over various disagreements.
item.dc:creator: SAMY MAGDY and TIA GOLDENBERG, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: 13 die as an Indian navy speedboat crashes into a passenger ferry off Mumbai
item.description: At least 13 people were killed after an Indian navy speedboat crashed into a ferry that was carrying more than 100 people; the ferry was on its way to a tourist destination off Mumbai.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Blinken says 'pressure is on Hamas' to move forward with ceasefire in Gaza
item.description: Secretary of State Antony Blinken said the pressure is on the Palestinian militant group Hamas to move forward with the ceasefire in the Gaza Strip, arguing the group’s support from its allies in the region has dwindled in recent months.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, The Hill

item.title: Canada's Trudeau to shuffle his Cabinet Friday amid resignation calls and rising discontent
item.description: TORONTO (AP) — Embattled Canadian Prime Minister Justin Trudeau will shuffle his Cabinet Friday, two senior officials familiar with the matter said Thursday. The officials confirmed the shuffle and spoke on condition of anonymity as they were not authorized to speak publicly on the matter.
item.dc:creator: ROB GILLIES, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Syrian villagers near the Golan Heights say Israeli forces are banning them from their fields
item.description: Israeli forces have set up a position in an abandoned Syrian army base in the village of Maariyah and prevented local farmers from accessing their fields, residents said Thursday.
item.dc:creator: GHAITH ALSAYED and HUSSEIN MALLA, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: 5 takeaways from Putin's annual press conference
item.description: Russian President Vladimir Putin talked for more than three hours during an annual news conference in Moscow on Thursday, discussing key topics including the war in Ukraine, his interest in dialogue with President-elect Trump and an openness to asking about detained journalist Austin Tice in Syria.
item.dc:creator: Brad Dress, The Hill

item.title: Blinken announces aid for Sudan and talks about AI risks at UN Security Council
item.description: Secretary of State Antony Blinken has announced $200 million in humanitarian aid for conflict-torn Sudan during his likely final trip to the United Nations while in office.
item.dc:creator: Edith M. Lederer And Matthew Lee, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Frenchman found guilty in horrific rape trial that shocked the world
item.description: A Frenchman accused of drugging his wife of 50 years and inviting strangers over to rape her – while he filmed the assaults – was found guilty on Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: China skirts US efforts to stiff-arm CCP interference by bolstering state, local relations
item.description: China is skirting U.S. efforts to block its interference in areas ranging from trade to national security by cozying up with state officials to foster relations and keep its influence active in the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: France's Macron says tour of cyclone-devastated Mayotte is a day he won't forget
item.description: French President Emmanuel Macron has traveled to the Indian Ocean archipelago of Mayotte to survey the devastation wreaked by Cyclone Chido.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Japanese newspaper boss who influenced the nation's postwar politics died at 98
item.description: TOKYO (AP) — Tsuneo Watanabe, the powerful head of Japan's largest newspaper who had close ties with the country's powerful conservative leaders, has died, his company said Thursday. He was 98.
item.dc:creator: MARI YAMAGUCHI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Number of dead and missing still unclear as first aid arrives in quake-hit Pacific nation of Vanuatu
item.description: The number of people killed and missing remains unclear two days after a magnitude 7.3 earthquake struck the Pacific island nation of Vanuatu.
item.dc:creator: Charlotte Graham-Mclay, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Gisèle Pelicot thanks backers after her ex-husband and his co-defendants are convicted in rape trial
item.description: Gisèle Pelicot said after 51 men were convicted in the drugging-and-rape trial that riveted France and turned her into an icon that the ordeal was “very difficult” and expressed support for other victims of sexual violence.
item.dc:creator: John Leicester, Tom Nouvian And Marine Lesprit, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Survivors recount 'very apocalyptic' cyclone that ripped through French territory of Mayotte
item.description: MAMOUDZOU, Mayotte (AP) — They clustered around water taps, rare sources of electricity and each other.
item.dc:creator: SAM MEDNICK, ADRIENNE SURPRENANT and SYLVIE CORBET, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Rescuers dig for survivors of Vanuatu earthquake with no water and unclear death toll
item.description: WELLINGTON, New Zealand (AP) — Vanuatu's capital was without water on Wednesday, a day after reservoirs were destroyed by a violent magnitude 7.3 earthquake that wrought havoc on the South Pacific island nation, with the number of people killed and injured expected to rise.
item.dc:creator: CHARLOTTE GRAHAM-McLAY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Biden envoy: Ukraine's future in NATO now in Trump's hands
item.description: A senior aide to President Biden said that the administration will leave up to the incoming Trump administration the next steps on Ukraine’s path to NATO membership, despite internal discussions on whether to push for the alliance to issue an invitation for Kyiv in the lame-duck session.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, The Hill

item.title: Cyclone Chido leaves Mayotte reeling. Warmer oceans fueled it
item.description: The Indian Ocean archipelago of Mayotte is reeling from Cyclone Chido, the most intense storm to hit the French territory in 90 years.
item.dc:creator: TAIWO ADEBAYO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Russia detains suspect accused of killing high-ranking general in Moscow
item.description: A suspect has been taken into custody in connection with the Tuesday killing of Lt. Gen. Igor Kirillov and his assistant in Moscow, according to Russian officials.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Bush, Obama alumni ask Biden administration to ease up on Cuba before Trump takes office
item.description: A group of former diplomatic and national security officials is calling on the Biden administration to loosen some restrictions on Cuba before handing over the reins of U.S. diplomacy to President-elect Trump.
item.dc:creator: Rafael Bernal, The Hill

item.title: Netanyahu says Israel will occupy border territory in Syria for now
item.description: Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu said Tuesday that his forces will for now occupy territory they had seized over the border in Syria.
item.dc:creator: Brad Dress, The Hill

item.title: Blast kills 2 Mexican soldiers and wounds 5 others in suspected booby trap by drug cartel
item.description: While approaching, they spotted three dismembered bodies near the encampment, which appeared to be abandoned. But as they drew closer, a buried device exploded and struck the soldiers.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: 11 Indian citizens dead in Georgian ski resort accident, embassy says
item.description: Eleven Indian citizens were among 12 victims of a deadly accident at an Indian restaurant in the Georgian ski resort of Gudauri, the Indian Embassy said.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Canada's finance minister resigns as unpopular Trudeau faces biggest test of his political career
item.description: TORONTO (AP) — Canadian Prime Minister Justin Trudeau faced the biggest test of his political career after Finance Minister Chrystia Freeland, long one of his most powerful and loyal ministers, resigned from the Cabinet on Monday.
item.dc:creator: ROB GILLIES, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Cartel-run clandestine fentanyl labs increasingly difficult to track
item.description: Mexican drug cartels are driving an opioid epidemic, flooding the United States with increasingly potent synthetic drugs.
item.dc:creator: Jorge Ventura, NewsNation

item.title: Ex-soccer player Kavelashvili becomes Georgia's president in a blow to country's EU aspirations
item.description: TBILISI, Georgia (AP) — Former soccer player Mikheil Kavelashvili became president of Georgia on Saturday, as the ruling party tightened its grip in what the opposition calls a blow to the country’s EU aspirations and a victory for former imperial ruler Russia.
item.dc:creator: SOPHIKO MEGRELIDZE, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: UN talks fail to reach agreement on dealing with rising risk of global drought
item.description: BENGALURU, India (AP) — Despite two weeks of U.N.-sponsored talks in Saudi Arabia’s Riyadh, the participating 197 nations failed to agree early Saturday on a plan to deal with global droughts, made longer and more severe by a warming climate.
item.dc:creator: SIBI ARASU, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Chinese phone hack victims haven't been notified: Report
item.description: AT&T and Verizon have reportedly not informed some hacking victims that they were specifically targeted.
item.dc:creator: NewsNation Staff, NewsNation

item.title: Middle East latest: Syrians celebrate Assad's fall as US seeks a peaceful political transition
item.description: Thousands of Syrians gathered in Damascus’ main square and a historic mosque for the first Muslim Friday prayers since former President Bashar Assad was overthrown, a major symbolic moment for the country’s dramatic change of power.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Saudi Arabia's plans to host the men's World Cup 2034 will be harmful for the climate, experts say
item.description: As the newly-named host of the 2034 World Cup in men's soccer, Saudi Arabia says it will construct or renovate 15 stadiums, create a futuristic city and expand airports in a massive buildout to accommodate millions of athletes, coaches and spectators.
item.dc:creator: ALEXA ST. JOHN and JENNIFER McDERMOTT, Associated Press, WGN 9
